description: Syntax Highlighting with Chroma - Colorize source code using Chroma lexers, 256-color ANSI output, and monokai theme

userStories.title: Extend Style type to support foreground/background colors and bold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the term.Style type to carry color and bold information so that the rendering pipeline can output colored text.
acceptanceCriteria: Change Style type in term/screen.go from uint8 to a struct with fields: fg (Color), bg (Color), reverse (bool), bold (bool)
Define Color type as int16 where -1 means 'default/no color' (ColorDefault constant) and 0-255 are 256-color palette indices
Add methods: Foreground(Color) Style, Background(Color) Style, Bold(bool) Style that return new Style values
Add accessor methods: Fg() Color, Bg() Color, IsBold() bool
StyleDefault zero value has ColorDefault for both fg and bg, false for reverse and bold
Existing Reverse(bool) and IsReverse() methods continue to work
Update cell struct comparison in terminal.go Show() if needed (Go struct == works for comparable fields)
Existing tests in term/terminal_test.go pass
New tests for Foreground(), Background(), Bold(), Fg(), Bg(), IsBold() methods pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a breaking change to Style. The cell struct in terminal.go uses Style, but since all fields are comparable, == still works for diff detection in Show(). All SetContent() call sites in main.go pass term.StyleDefault or style.Reverse(true), which continue to work.

userStories.title: Update terminal rendering to output 256-color ANSI sequences
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the terminal's Show() method to emit ANSI color escape sequences so that styled cells appear in color.
acceptanceCriteria: Show() emits \033[38;5;Nm for foreground color N when a cell's fg is not ColorDefault
Show() emits \033[48;5;Nm for background color N when a cell's bg is not ColorDefault
Show() emits \033[1m for bold when a cell has bold enabled
Show() emits \033[7m for reverse video when a cell has reverse enabled
Show() emits \033[0m to reset all attributes after each styled cell (any cell that has non-default style)
Cells with only reverse video (no colors) continue to work as before
Cells with both color and reverse video render correctly (colors visible through reverse)
Cells with StyleDefault (no attributes) emit no escape sequences beyond cursor positioning
Existing terminal rendering tests pass; new test for color ANSI output passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build the ANSI sequence as: reset first if needed, then set all attributes (bold, fg, bg, reverse) in one batch before the character, and reset after. This minimizes escape sequence overhead.

userStories.title: Add Chroma-based syntax highlighting module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that tokenizes buffer content using Chroma and produces a per-cell style map so that drawWindowContent can apply syntax colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Run 'go get github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2' to add Chroma as a dependency
Create new file highlight.go in the main (goomacs) package
Define Highlighter struct holding a chroma.Lexer, a *chroma.Style (monokai), and cached result [][]term.Style
NewHighlighter(filename string) creates a highlighter by calling lexers.Match(filename); returns nil if no lexer matches the file extension

Highlight(lines [][]rune) method: joins lines into a single string, tokenizes with lexer.Tokenise(), iterates tokens mapping each token's chroma style color to term.Style with Foreground set to nearest 256-color index, and bold if the chroma style entry has Bold; stores result as [][]term.Style
StyleAt(row, col int) term.Style returns the cached highlight style at the given position, or term.StyleDefault if out of range
Use chroma/styles.Get('monokai') for the theme
Use the chroma Color type's method to convert RGB to nearest 256-color palette index
go build succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Chroma v2 API: lexers.Match(filename) for auto-detection, chroma.Coalesce(lexer) to merge tokens, styles.Get('monokai') for theme. Token iteration: lexer.Tokenise(nil, source) returns an Iterator; call Tokens() to get []Token. Each Token has Type and Value; use style.Get(token.Type) to get the chroma.StyleEntry with Colour and Bold fields. Use Colour.Nearest256() or manual mapping for 256-color index.

userStories.title: Add highlight dirty flag to Buffer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dirty flag on Buffer so that re-highlighting only happens when content changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add HighlightDirty bool field to Buffer struct in buffer.go
Set HighlightDirty = true in all edit methods that modify Lines: InsertChar, InsertNewline, Backspace, DeleteChar, KillLine, KillRegion, Yank, Undo
NewBuffer() and NewBufferFromFile() set HighlightDirty = true initially
go test ./... passes (existing buffer tests still pass)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a simple addition to buffer.go. The flag is set in edit methods and will be checked/cleared by the rendering pipeline in the next story.

userStories.title: Integrate highlighting into buffer and rendering pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want source code files to appear with syntax colors so that I can read code more easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Highlight *Highlighter field to Buffer struct
When NewBufferFromFile() loads a file, create a Highlighter via NewHighlighter(filename) and attach it to the buffer (nil if no lexer matches)
When C-x C-f opens a new file, the new buffer gets a Highlighter attached
In drawWindowContent(), before rendering: if buf.HighlightDirty and buf.Highlight is not nil, call buf.Highlight.Highlight(buf.Lines) and set buf.HighlightDirty = false
In drawWindowContent(), for each cell: get base style from highlighter via StyleAt(bufRow, bufCol), then apply reverse video on top if the cell is in a region or search match
Files without a matching Chroma lexer render in default terminal color (no error, no crash)
Special buffers (*scratch*, *Buffer List*) have nil Highlighter and render normally
Opening a .go file shows Go syntax highlighting (keywords, strings, comments in distinct colors)
Update README.md: change 'Zero dependencies' to 'Minimal dependencies' or similar, add 'Syntax highlighting' to features list
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The key rendering logic change is in drawWindowContent(): instead of always using term.StyleDefault as the base style, use highlighter.StyleAt(row, col) when available. Region/search reverse video is applied on top via style.Reverse(true). The HighlightDirty flag from US-004 prevents re-tokenizing on every redraw.
